locations.name: Garden of Eden
locations.notes: Commonly placed near confluence of Tigris/Euphrates in southern Mesopotamia. Location debated.

locations.name: Pishon River
locations.notes: One of four Eden rivers. Identified variously with Karun River, Wadi Batin, or lost watercourse.

locations.name: Land of Havilah
locations.notes: Usually associated with Arabia or Persian Gulf region. Multiple biblical Havilahs may exist.

locations.name: Gihon River
locations.notes: One of four Eden rivers. Sometimes identified with Nile or a Mesopotamian watercourse. Surrounds Cush.

locations.name: Cush
locations.notes: Ancient Nubia/northern Sudan region, not modern Ethiopia. Well-attested in Egyptian records.

locations.name: Tigris River
locations.notes: Well-known river. Hebrew: Hiddekel. Flows east of Assyria.

locations.name: Assyria
locations.notes: Northern Mesopotamia, centered on Nineveh/Mosul. Well-attested archaeological region.

locations.name: Euphrates River
locations.notes: Well-known river. Fourth river of Eden. Major landmark in Abrahamic covenant (Gen 15:18).

locations.name: Land of Nod
locations.notes: East of Eden. Name means 'wandering'. No archaeological identification. Coordinates speculative.

locations.name: Mountains of Ararat
locations.notes: Traditional identification with Mt. Ararat in eastern Turkey. Biblical Ararat = kingdom of Urartu.

locations.name: Land of Shinar
locations.notes: Southern Mesopotamia / Babylonia. Setting of Tower of Babel.

locations.name: Babel / Babylon
locations.notes: Well-attested archaeological site south of modern Baghdad.

locations.name: Erech / Uruk
locations.notes: Modern Warka, Iraq. Major Sumerian city, well-excavated.

locations.name: Akkad / Accad
locations.notes: Capital of Akkadian Empire. Exact site not securely identified. Somewhere in central Mesopotamia.

locations.name: Calneh
locations.notes: Debated identification. Possibly Nippur or Kullani. Some scholars read 'all of them' instead of a place name.

locations.name: Nineveh
locations.notes: Modern Mosul area, Iraq. Extensively excavated Assyrian capital.

locations.name: Rehoboth-Ir
locations.notes: Near Nineveh. May mean 'broad places of the city' (describing Nineveh) rather than a separate city.

locations.name: Calah / Nimrud
locations.notes: Modern Nimrud, Iraq. Major Assyrian city, well-excavated.

locations.name: Resen
locations.notes: Between Nineveh and Calah. Not securely identified.

locations.name: Sidon
locations.notes: Modern Saida, Lebanon. Major Phoenician port city.

locations.name: Sodom
locations.notes: Destroyed city. Usually placed near southern Dead Sea. Bab edh-Dhra is one candidate.

locations.name: Gomorrah
locations.notes: Destroyed city. Near Sodom. Numeira is one candidate near southern Dead Sea.

locations.name: Admah
locations.notes: One of the five Cities of the Plain. No secure identification.

locations.name: Zeboiim
locations.notes: One of the five Cities of the Plain. No secure identification.

locations.name: Zoar
locations.notes: Near southern Dead Sea. Identified with es-Safi or nearby. Spared from destruction of the Plain cities.

locations.name: Lasha
locations.notes: Boundary marker of Canaanite territory. Sometimes identified with Callirrhoe (hot springs east of Dead Sea).

locations.name: Land of Canaan
locations.notes: Well-defined ancient region. Centered coordinate approximates central highlands.

locations.name: Egypt
locations.notes: Well-known. Coordinate centered on Memphis/Cairo area, the ancient capital region.

locations.name: Ur of the Chaldeans
locations.notes: Tell el-Muqayyar, southern Iraq. Major Sumerian city, well-excavated.

locations.name: Haran
locations.notes: Modern Harran, southeastern Turkey. Well-attested archaeological site on ancient trade routes.

locations.name: Shechem
locations.notes: Tell Balata, near modern Nablus. Extensively excavated.

locations.name: Oak/Terebinth of Moreh
locations.notes: Near Shechem. Sacred tree site where Abraham built an altar.

locations.name: Bethel
locations.notes: Traditionally identified with Beitin, some scholars prefer el-Bireh. Major patriarchal and later Israelite site.

locations.name: Ai
locations.notes: East of Bethel. Usually identified with et-Tell, though Khirbet el-Maqatir is debated alternative.

locations.name: The Negev
locations.notes: Southern desert region of Canaan. Well-defined geographic area.

locations.name: Jordan River / Valley
locations.notes: Well-known river and rift valley.

locations.name: Valley of Siddim
locations.notes: Identified with the Dead Sea basin, possibly the shallow southern end before flooding.

locations.name: Salem
locations.notes: Identified with Jerusalem. Melchizedek's city. Psalm 76:2 confirms Salem = Zion.

locations.name: Dan
locations.notes: Tell Dan (Tell el-Qadi), northern Israel. Well-excavated. Anachronistic name in Gen 14.

locations.name: Hobah
locations.notes: North of Damascus. Abraham pursued kings here. Possibly modern Hoba near Damascus.

locations.name: Damascus
locations.notes: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities. Well-known location.

locations.name: El-Paran
locations.notes: At the edge of the wilderness. Possibly modern Eilat/Aqaba area.

locations.name: Kadesh / En-Mishpat
locations.notes: Usually identified with Ain el-Qudeirat in the northeastern Sinai.

locations.name: Hazazon-Tamar
locations.notes: Usually identified with En-Gedi (2 Chr 20:2). Oasis on western Dead Sea shore.

locations.name: Wilderness of Shur
locations.notes: Northwestern Sinai region, east of Egypt. On the road from Egypt to Canaan.

locations.name: Beer-lahai-roi
aliases: the well of the Living One who sees me
locations.notes: Between Kadesh and Bered in the Negev. Hagar's well. Exact site unknown.

locations.name: Bered
locations.notes: Near Beer-lahai-roi in the Negev. No secure identification.

locations.name: Mamre / Oaks of Mamre
locations.notes: Near Hebron. Identified with Ramat el-Khalil, 3km north of Hebron. Abraham's main dwelling place.

locations.name: Hebron
locations.notes: Modern al-Khalil/Hebron. One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

locations.name: Cave of Machpelah
locations.notes: Tomb of the Patriarchs in Hebron. Traditionally identified site beneath the Ibrahimi Mosque.

locations.name: Gerar
locations.notes: Philistine city. Tell Abu Hureyra or Tell Haror are candidates. In the western Negev.

locations.name: Beer-sheba
locations.notes: Tell es-Saba / modern Be'er Sheva. Southern boundary marker of Israel. Well-excavated.

locations.name: Wilderness of Paran
locations.notes: Central Sinai peninsula region. Ishmael settled here.

locations.name: Mount Moriah
locations.notes: Traditional identification with Temple Mount in Jerusalem (2 Chr 3:1). Some scholars debate.

locations.name: Aram-Naharaim
locations.notes: Upper Mesopotamia between Euphrates and Habur/Balikh rivers. Region around Haran.

locations.name: Paddan-Aram
locations.notes: Region around Haran in upper Mesopotamia. Name possibly means 'field of Aram'.

locations.name: Esek
locations.notes: Well dug by Isaac's servants near Gerar. Name means 'contention'. No secure identification.

locations.name: Sitnah
locations.notes: Second well dug by Isaac near Gerar. Name means 'accusation'. No secure identification.

locations.name: Rehoboth (well)
locations.notes: Third well dug by Isaac. Name means 'broad places'. Possibly Ruhaibeh in Negev.

locations.name: Gilead
locations.notes: Transjordan highland region east of the Jordan. Modern northwestern Jordan.

locations.name: Mizpah of Gilead
locations.notes: Cairn marking covenant between Jacob and Laban. In Gilead. Galeed = Hebrew name, Jegar-Sahadutha = Aramaic.

locations.name: Mahanaim
locations.notes: Where Jacob saw angels. Usually identified with Tulul ed-Dhahab near the Jabbok. Name means 'two camps'.

locations.name: Mount Seir / Land of Seir
locations.notes: Mountain range in southern Jordan/Edom. Esau's territory. Identified with mountains east of Arabah.

locations.name: Edom
locations.notes: Esau's territory, southern Transjordan. Overlaps with Seir.

locations.name: Peniel / Penuel
locations.notes: Where Jacob wrestled with God. Near the Jabbok crossing. Name means 'face of God'.

locations.name: Jabbok River
locations.notes: Modern Zarqa River in Jordan. Major Transjordan tributary of the Jordan.

locations.name: Succoth
locations.notes: In Jordan Valley. Usually identified with Tell Deir Alla area. Name means 'booths'.

locations.name: Shalem
locations.notes: Gen 33:18 'Jacob came shalem to the city of Shechem'. Debated whether this is a place name or means 'safely'.

locations.name: Ephrath / Bethlehem
locations.notes: Modern Bethlehem. Rachel's burial place. Well-attested.

locations.name: Migdal-Eder
locations.notes: Near Bethlehem. 'Tower of the flock'. Exact location debated.

locations.name: Dothan
locations.notes: Tell Dothan. Well-excavated site where Joseph was sold by his brothers.

locations.name: Adullam
locations.notes: In the Shephelah. Judah's friend Hirah was from here. Tell es-Sheikh Madhkur is one candidate.

locations.name: Timnah
locations.notes: Tell Batash in the Sorek Valley. Where Judah went for sheep-shearing.

locations.name: Enaim
locations.notes: On the road to Timnah. Where Tamar sat. No secure identification.

locations.name: Land of Goshen
locations.notes: Eastern Nile Delta region. Best pastureland in Egypt. Wadi Tumilat area.

locations.name: Rameses
locations.notes: Tell el-Dab'a / Qantir area. Pi-Ramesses, Ramesside capital in the eastern Delta.

locations.name: Abel-Mizraim
locations.notes: Beyond the Jordan. Mourning place during Jacob's funeral procession. Name means 'mourning of Egypt'.

locations.name: Land of the Philistines
locations.notes: Coastal plain of southern Canaan. Anachronistic for patriarchal period but well-attested later.

locations.name: River of Egypt
locations.notes: Southern boundary of promised land (Gen 15:18). Usually Wadi el-Arish, not the Nile.

locations.name: Shaveh-Kiriathaim
locations.notes: In Moab. Where the Emim were defeated. Possibly near Kiriathaim in Transjordan.

locations.name: King's Valley / Valley of Shaveh
locations.notes: Near Salem/Jerusalem. Where Abraham met Melchizedek. Traditionally the Kidron Valley.

locations.name: On / Heliopolis
locations.notes: Ancient Egyptian religious center. Modern Ain Shams/Matariyyah in Cairo. Joseph's father-in-law was priest here.
